  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which primary emphasis should this review take: (A) technical correctness and claim–evidence alignment (logic, guarantees, experiments), (B) writing polish only (grammar, clarity, concision), (C) section-level structure and narrative flow, or (D) novelty/positioning vs prior work?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The section asserts protocol properties and guarantees, so validating the internal logic from premises to conclusions is load-bearing.",
    "R1.3": "It reports latency and availability results, making the alignment between these data and the stated claims a central review task.",
    "R1.6": "A typical section review includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, tone, and concision improvements.",
    "RX.1": "The review should note uncertainties due to missing referenced sections (e.g., Sections 3–5) and limited context.",
    "RX.2": "The review must avoid amplifying unsubstantiated assertions (e.g., 'novel') beyond what the excerpt supports.",
    "RX.3": "Feedback will be constrained to the provided text rather than the full paper or implementation details not shown.",
    "RX.4": "Comments will avoid encouraging misleading or unsafe claims about consistency or availability in distributed settings.",
    "RX.5": "Each point will be traceable to specific sentences or claims within this excerpt without relying on external, uncited sources."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which primary emphasis should this review take: (A) technical correctness and claim–evidence alignment (logic, guarantees, experiments), (B) writing polish only (grammar, clarity, concision), (C) section-level structure and narrative flow, or (D) novelty/positioning vs prior work?

rationale.R1.2: The section asserts protocol properties and guarantees, so validating the internal logic from premises to conclusions is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: It reports latency and availability results, making the alignment between these data and the stated claims a central review task.
rationale.R1.6: A typical section review includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, tone, and concision improvements.
rationale.RX.1: The review should note uncertainties due to missing referenced sections (e.g., Sections 3–5) and limited context.
rationale.RX.2: The review must avoid amplifying unsubstantiated assertions (e.g., 'novel') beyond what the excerpt supports.
rationale.RX.3: Feedback will be constrained to the provided text rather than the full paper or implementation details not shown.
rationale.RX.4: Comments will avoid encouraging misleading or unsafe claims about consistency or availability in distributed settings.
rationale.RX.5: Each point will be traceable to specific sentences or claims within this excerpt without relying on external, uncited sources.
